author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
It is to Be Wondered at that Men Should Rather Trust to Their Own Weakness Than to God’s Strength.
title: It is to Be Wondered at that Men Should Rather Trust to Their Own Weakness Than to God’s Strength.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Certainly, when the apostle says, “Therefore it is of faith that the promise may be sure according to grace,” I marvel that men would rather entrust themselves to their own weakness, than to the strength of God’s promise.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But sayest thou, God’s will concerning myself is to me uncertain?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Is thine own will concerning thyself certain to thee? and dost thou not fear,—“Let him that thinketh he standeth take heed lest he fall”?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Since, then, both are uncertain, why does not man commit his faith, hope, and love to the stronger will rather than to the weaker?
